title: Smart Battery Management System Monitor
description: Arduino-based BMS monitor for a 3S Li-Ion or LiFePO₄ pack. Measures individual cell voltages via resistive dividers, total pack current via INA219, and estimates state-of-charge (SoC) by Coulomb counting. Displays live data on OLED and alerts on over/under-voltage or SoC < 10 %.
objective: Monitor 3-cell pack (3S Li-Ion, 12.6 V max); SoC accuracy ±5 %; cell voltage resolution ≤ 10 mV; alert on cell > 4.2 V or < 3.0 V; USB serial logging at 1 Hz.
constraints: 5 V USB power from charger port; ≤ £15 BOM; single Arduino Nano; I²C for INA219 and OLED.
supporting_fact_titles: Li-Ion Battery Capacity and Energy Calculation
OLED SSD1306 Monochrome Display I²C Interface
design_element_titles: Resistive Cell Voltage Sensing Divider Array
INA219 I²C Current and Power Monitor

element_usage_notes.Resistive Cell Voltage Sensing Divider A: Three 2-resistor dividers (47 kΩ / 100 kΩ) on A0, A1, A2 for cells 1, 2, 3. 0.1 µF ceramic cap at each ADC pin. VREF = 3.3 V (Nano 3.3 V pin via AREF).
element_usage_notes.INA219 I²C Current and Power Monitor: 0.1 Ω shunt on pack negative rail; I²C addr 0x40; polled at 10 Hz; current integrated for Coulomb counting.
element_usage_notes.SSD1306 OLED Display UI Module: 4-line layout: SoC% + pack voltage / cell voltages 1–3 / current mA / status. 1 Hz refresh.
element_usage_notes.Arduino Uno/Nano MCU Platform: Nano 3.0; AREF wired to 3.3 V pin for 10-bit ADC ref; I²C on A4/A5 (INA219 + OLED); buzzer on D7.

element_interactions.from: Resistive Cell Voltage Sensing Divider Array
element_interactions.data: Analog voltages on A0, A1, A2 (0–2.86 V representing 0–4.2 V cell)
element_interactions.description: Nano samples each ADC channel 8× and averages; converts via divider ratio (×1.468) to actual cell voltage. Flags over/under-voltage conditions for buzzer.

element_interactions.from: INA219 I²C Current and Power Monitor
element_interactions.data: current_mA (float), bus_v (float) via I²C at 10 Hz
element_interactions.description: INA219 polled at 10 Hz. current_mA integrated with 100 ms time step to accumulate charge (mAh). SoC = Q_remaining / Q_nominal × 100 %.

element_interactions.data: I²C framebuffer update (soc_pct, cell_v[3], current_ma, status_str)
element_interactions.description: OLED updated at 1 Hz with current SoC bar, per-cell voltages, and alarm status icon.
